verdict_reason: This is the verse where I expected potential failure — nistrai-guṇya is metaphysically loaded, and most engineering layers would either flatten it to 'be calm' (trivialisation) or overclaim by saying engineers can 'transcend' something. This layer does neither. It explicitly tags the analog STRETCHED, sets confidence to MEDIUM, and writes: 'this analog does NOT claim engineers can transcend ontology. It claims they can rise above team-state-fluctuation, which is a small operational reflection of a much larger metaphysical injunction.' That is the correct epistemic move. Both Shankara's reading (Vedas' karma-kāṇḍa takes worldly-life-domain as its object; nitya-sattva-stha read as 'reality' to resolve apparent contradiction) and Ramanuja's reading (the karma-kāṇḍa addresses guṇa-preponderant persons; nitya-sattva-stha as a stage of pure-sattva preponderance) are recorded without conflation. The transliterated technical terms (nistrai-guṇya, nirdvandva, nitya-sattva-stha, niryoga-kṣema, ātmavān, sattva, rajas, tamas, guṇa) are all preserved load-bearing. The most subtle move — that 'transcend the three guṇas' is not 'have a good attitude' — is preserved. PASS for both 7.4 and 6.8.
